visualGoal: Make a PC builder want the 12Nm sweet-spot rig before they read the torque-truth caveats. Real wheelbase product proof throughout; no procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Best-budget pick, currently text-only in body because no verified image. Needs a real base photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid or be certified.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic Gen X garage sim-racing bench at golden-hour rim light, a rigid 8020 wheel-stand with an empty 4-bolt mounting plate left open for a real wheelbase product overlay, cable tray visible, curator Duke Alvarez gesturing at the mount point, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, one hand on a direct-drive wheelbase mount, confident mechanic energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
